researchSkipReason: Driver family fixed by sibling consistency (fabric uses tokio-postgres); only version verification (Tier 3) was needed. No discovery/skeleton search warranted for a thin two-query adapter.

candidates.name: tokio-postgres
candidates.evidence: Registry-verified 0.7.18. flint-realtime-fabric uses tokio-postgres 0.7 with with-serde_json-1; sibling-consistent (Base Rule 9). Async, native, binds serde_json::Value for the JSONB body.

candidates.name: deadpool-postgres
candidates.evidence: Registry-verified 0.14.1. Idiomatic async pool over tokio-postgres; the agent is a long-lived server and needs pooling.

candidates.name: testcontainers + testcontainers-modules
candidates.role: dev-dep: ephemeral Postgres for the restart-survival test
candidates.evidence: Registry-verified. Test is #[ignore]d-by-default (needs Docker, unavailable in this env).

candidates.name: sqlx
candidates.evidence: Compile-time query checking needs a live DB or offline metadata at build time — CI friction and a different driver family than the sibling. Not worth it for a two-query adapter.

build_required.summary: New fpa-store-pg adapter crate implementing ProjectStore over tokio-postgres+deadpool; Project stored whole as JSONB in a single agent-owned table; composition root picks Pg-vs-InMem by FPA_PROJECT_DB_URL
Archive p5-c001..c004 OpenSpec changes into specs/ (housekeeping)
